From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

West Germanic language of Nepali dialect spoken by Nepali people

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Nepalese_English "Special:EditPage/Nepalese English") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). | [Language family](/wiki/Language_family "Language family") | [Indo-European](/wiki/Indo-European_languages "Indo-European languages")  * [Germanic](/wiki/Germanic_languages "Germanic languages")   + [West Germanic](/wiki/West_Germanic_languages "West Germanic languages")     - [Ingvaeonic](/wiki/Ingvaeonic_languages "Ingvaeonic languages")       * [Anglo-Frisian](/wiki/Anglo-Frisian_languages "Anglo-Frisian languages")         + [Anglic](/wiki/Anglic_languages "Anglic languages")           - [English](/wiki/English_language "English language")             * [Asian English](/wiki/Asian_English "Asian English")               + [South Asian English](/wiki/South_Asian_English "South Asian English")                 - **Nepalese English** |
| Early forms | [Proto-Indo-European](/wiki/Proto-Indo-European_language "Proto-Indo-European language")  * [Proto-Germanic](/wiki/Proto-Germanic_language "Proto-Germanic language")   + [Proto-English](/wiki/Proto-English "Proto-English")     - [Old English](/wiki/Old_English "Old English")       * [Middle English](/wiki/Middle_English "Middle English")         + [Early Modern English](/wiki/Early_Modern_English "Early Modern English")           - [Modern English](/wiki/Modern_English "Modern English") |
| [Writing system](/wiki/Writing_system "Writing system") | [Latin](/wiki/Latin_script "Latin script") |
| Language codes | |
| [ISO 639-3](/wiki/ISO_639-3 "ISO 639-3") | – |
| [IETF](/wiki/IETF_language_tag "IETF language tag") | `en-NP` |

**Nepalese English** refers to a variety of the [English language](/wiki/English_language "English language") principally used in [Nepal](/wiki/Nepal "Nepal") as well as neighboring [Sikkim](/wiki/Sikkim "Sikkim") and [Gorkhaland](/wiki/Gorkhaland "Gorkhaland") regions of India. It is heavily influenced by the [Indo-Aryan languages](/wiki/Indo-Aryan_languages "Indo-Aryan languages") of Nepal.

Many Nepalese speak [English as a second or foreign language](/wiki/English_as_a_second_or_foreign_language "English as a second or foreign language"), with English use being most prevalent among city dwellers residing in [Kathmandu](/wiki/Kathmandu "Kathmandu") (the capital of Nepal). Although [Nepali](/wiki/Nepali_language "Nepali language") is the native language, English is the primary language used for business in Nepal. In Nepal, where modern English education began in the 1850s, there is little or no consensus among teachers and practitioners on whether to follow [British](/wiki/British_English "British English"), [American](/wiki/American_English "American English") or [Indian](/wiki/Indian_English "Indian English") variants of English, or allow the development of a Nepal-specific variety of English.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Colloquially, code-mixed Nepali and English is known as **Nenglish** (a term first recorded in 1999), or, less commonly, as **Nepanglish** (2000) or **Neplish** (2002).[[2]](#cite_note-2)
